name: Integral del Salto de Roldán Trail
name_original: Santa Eulalia de la Peña y Salto de Roldán desde Huesca
description: This moderate 28.9-mile mountain bike route from Huesca explores Santa Eulalia de la Peña and the stunning Salto de Roldán.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Huesca offers diverse terrain across the Aragonese Pyrenees and Pre-Pyrenees. The region features a dramatic landscape of mountain ranges, deep valleys, and canyons, with some trails reaching high altitudes. Notable natural features include the unique rock formations of the Kingdom of the Mallos and the water-sculpted landscapes of Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park. This varied topography provides an extensive network of trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Huesca The most popular mountain bike trail is Huesca and the Reservoirs of Sierra de Guara Loop, a difficult 36.1 miles (58.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 59 minutes to complete. This route explores the varied landscapes around the Sierra de Guara reservoirs. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Integral del Salto de Roldán Trail, a moderate 28.9 miles (46.5 km) path. This trail offers views of the iconic Salto de Roldán rock formation. Local mountain bikers also love the Salto Roldán Viewpoint – Apiés loop from Huesca, a 48.5 miles (78.1 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and past viewpoints, often completed in about 6 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Huesca is defined by mountainous terrain, deep canyons, and unique rock formations. The network offers options for all ability levels, from challenging enduro routes to more accessible paths. The routes in Huesca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 1600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Huesca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Huesca?
A: There are over 140 mountain bike trails around Huesca, catering to various skill levels. This includes more than 30 easy routes, over 100 moderate trails, and 17 challenging options for experienced riders.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Huesca?
A: Mountain biking in Huesca offers incredibly diverse terrain. You'll encounter dramatic landscapes of the Aragonese Pyrenees and Pre-Pyrenees, including mountain ranges, deep valleys, and canyons. Unique rock formations like the Mallos de Riglos and Mallos de Agüero, as well as water-sculpted areas in the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, define many routes. The region features an extensive network of tracks, trails, and technical descents.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Huesca?
A: Yes, Huesca offers options suitable for families. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed in the provided data, the region has over 30 easy trails. The Gállego Greenway, mentioned in regional research, is also noted as a suitable option for cycling with children, offering a more relaxed experience.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Huesca?
A: The mountain bike trails around Huesca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. Riders frequently praise the varied terrain, from mountainous landscapes to unique rock formations and deep canyons, and the extensive network that offers options for all ability levels.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Huesca?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Huesca are circular. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964629592/huesca-and-the-reservoirs-of-sierra-de-guara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huesca and the Reservoirs of Sierra de Guara Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you through varied landscapes. Another popular option is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto Roldán Viewpoint – Apiés loop from Huesca&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through mountainous terrain and past viewpoints.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for advanced riders in Huesca?
A: For advanced riders, Huesca offers several challenging options. The region is home to areas like Zona Zero in Aínsa and Puro Pirineo in Castejón de Sos, known for their demanding trails. Specific routes include the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964629592/huesca-and-the-reservoirs-of-sierra-de-guara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huesca and the Reservoirs of Sierra de Guara Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 58.2 km with significant elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto Roldán Viewpoint – Apiés loop from Huesca&lt;/a&gt;, a 78.1 km route with over 1000m of ascent.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along mountain bike routes in Huesca?
A: Huesca's mountain bike routes often pass by stunning natural attractions. You can explore areas like the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, known for its water-sculpted landscapes. Routes near the Sierra de Guara reservoirs offer views of these bodies of water, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadiello Dam&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadiello Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The iconic Salto de Roldán rock formation is also a prominent feature on trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976126819/integral-del-salto-de-roldan-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Integral del Salto de Roldán Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Salto de Roldán?
A: Yes, the Salto de Roldán rock formation is a significant landmark in the Huesca region, and several mountain bike trails offer views of it. The moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976126819/integral-del-salto-de-roldan-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Integral del Salto de Roldán Trail&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to provide views of this iconic formation. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19887055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huesca Cathedral – Salto Roldán Viewpoint loop from Huesca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in Huesca?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Huesca varies widely depending on their length and difficulty. For example, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24313844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trenches Route – Plaza de Luis López Allué loop from Huesca&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 24.8 km, can take around 2 hours 6 minutes. Longer, more difficult trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto Roldán Viewpoint – Apiés loop from Huesca&lt;/a&gt; (78.1 km), typically require around 6 hours 35 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that pass by reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, several mountain bike trails in Huesca feature reservoirs and lakes. The difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964629592/huesca-and-the-reservoirs-of-sierra-de-guara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huesca and the Reservoirs of Sierra de Guara Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores the varied landscapes around the Sierra de Guara reservoirs. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadiello Dam&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadiello Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; in the region, which are often accessible or visible from various routes.

Q: What are some notable geological features to see while mountain biking in Huesca?
A: Huesca is renowned for its unique geological features. The 'Kingdom of the Mallos' is characterized by massive rock formations like the Mallos de Riglos and Mallos de Agüero, which provide a stunning backdrop for cycling. The Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park also showcases majestic water-sculpted landscapes, including deep, narrow canyons and gorges such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5040942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgas de San Julián&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349400" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Upper Guatizalema Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: This place is known as Santolarieta in Aragonese.

Spectacular viewpoint over the Peña San Miguel, Pico el Fraile and Peña Amán that form the so-called Salto de Roldán. The area is impressive with cliffs, precipices and continual flying over by birds of prey that nest on these walls. Access to the viewpoint is restricted for vehicles, but it is allowed for people with disabilities.

It's true, it's magnificent... But either I took the wrong path, or this route should not be classified as intermediate, but expert. For a 3km stretch, I had to carry the bike most of the time, with the vultures circling above you and the ravine below.
So yes, I think there are at least 3km VERY difficult and caution is required (unless I took the wrong path, of course...)

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate, but it's important to note that the terrain is generally considered to have high physical and technical difficulty, making it best suited for experienced mountain bikers. Expect demanding ascents, notable descents, and sections described as 'roto' (broken) and 'chunky' with technical singletrack.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Santa Eulalia de la Peña y Salto de Roldán route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, including a mix of roads, tracks, and a substantial percentage of technical singletrack. You'll encounter challenging sections, including a 'tremendous' singletrack under reddish cliffs, which is a highlight for enduro enthusiasts.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers impressive views of the Pre-Pyrenees and the Hoya de Huesca basin. Key highlights include the village of Santa Eulalia de la Peña, the spectacular Salto Roldán Viewpoint, and riding along the Senda del Salto Roldán. The 'Salto de Roldán' itself is a geological wonder with two immense rock formations, Peña San Miguel and Peña Amán.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The recommended seasons for mountain biking this route are autumn, winter, and spring. It's best to avoid periods of heavy snow or significant rain, which can make the already challenging terrain even more difficult.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this area?
faqs.answer: The route is located within the Parque Natural de la Sierra y los Cañones de Guara. While there are generally no entrance fees for the park itself, it's always advisable to check local regulations for any specific permits required for certain activities or areas, especially if you plan to deviate from established trails.

faqs.question: Is the Santa Eulalia de la Peña y Salto de Roldán route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this route passes through a protected natural area known for its wildlife, including a large colony of griffon vultures, it's crucial to keep dogs under strict control, preferably on a leash, to protect the local fauna and ensure their safety. Always follow park regulations regarding pets.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, access points for routes in this area are typically found near Huesca or the village of Santa Eulalia de la Peña. It's recommended to check local maps or komoot's route planner for the most convenient starting points with parking facilities.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The area is renowned for its rich birdlife, particularly a large colony of griffon vultures that inhabit the rocky cliffs around Salto de Roldán. You'll have excellent opportunities to observe these majestic birds soaring overhead.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with sections of several other trails. You'll find it passes through parts of the BTT 15 Circuito BTT competición de Quicena, Recorrido por el Saso de Montearagón, Un paseo hasta las Fuentes de Marcelo, and the Camino de Santiago por Huesca, among others.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for mountain biking this 46.5 km route is approximately 4 hours and 9 minutes. However, this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, technical skill, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic vistas?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. From elevated points along the route, especially near the Salto Roldán Viewpoint, you can enjoy breathtaking panoramic vistas of the Pre-Pyrenees and the Hoya de Huesca. On clear days, these views can extend as far as the Moncayo and other ranges of the Iberian System.
